  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

Fig 1 G,I,K

packages <- c(
  "dplyr",
  "tidyr",
  "stringr",
  "pheatmap",
  "Matrix",
  "patchwork",
  "ggplot2",
  "tibble",
  "reshape2",
  "RColorBrewer",
  "arrow"
)

if (!requireNamespace("pak", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("pak")
}
library(pak)
pak::repo_add(CRAN = "https://packagemanager.posit.co/cran/2024-04-01")

invisible(lapply(packages, function(pkg) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) pak::pkg_install(pkg)
}))

invisible(lapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE))

Attaching package: 'dplyr'
The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag
The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union

Attaching package: 'Matrix'
The following objects are masked from 'package:tidyr':

    expand, pack, unpack

Attaching package: 'reshape2'
The following object is masked from 'package:tidyr':

    smiths

Attaching package: 'arrow'
The following object is masked from 'package:utils':

    timestamp
FIGURES_DIR <- "Figures"
dir.create(FIGURES_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE)

Read metadata

# all_obs.parquet can be downloaded from HF Hub via scripts/download.py
# See README.md for details
BASE_DIR <- "/data/wholebrain_crispr_atlas"
METADATA_PATH <- file.path(BASE_DIR, "metadata", "all_obs.parquet")

if (!file.exists(METADATA_PATH)) {
  stop(
    "Metadata file not found: ", METADATA_PATH, "\n",
    "all_obs.parquet can be downloaded from HF Hub via scripts/download.py. See README.md for details."
  )
}

meta <- read_parquet(METADATA_PATH)
cat(sprintf("Total nuclei: %s\n", format(nrow(meta), big.mark = ",")))
Total nuclei: 7,720,300
cat(sprintf("Columns: %s\n", paste(colnames(meta), collapse = ", ")))
Columns: cell_id, batch, scp_name, source, sex, sample_label, num_rna_umi, num_genes, pct_mt, scDblFinder.class, scDblFinder.score, log_ambient_mse, log_ambient_mse_norm, gene_target, num_guides, guide_call, guide_umis, guide_umi_top, guide_umi_second, neuron_type, neighborhood, predicted_group, predicted_class, predicted_class_probability, predicted_subclass, predicted_subclass_probability, predicted_supertype, predicted_supertype_probability, predicted_cluster, predicted_cluster_probability, cluster, passes_qc

Fig 1G

meta_f <- meta %>% filter(num_genes >= 2000) %>% filter(scDblFinder.class == 'singlet') %>% filter(log_ambient_mse_norm > 0.09)

# FIX: keep only the primary (first) neighborhood instead of expanding compound-neighborhood
# cells into multiple rows — avoids double-counting and inflated violin shapes.
meta_n <- meta_f %>%
  mutate(neighborhood = str_split_i(neighborhood, ";", 1) %>% str_trim())

qual_col_pals = brewer.pal.info[brewer.pal.info$category == 'qual',]
col_vector = unlist(mapply(brewer.pal, qual_col_pals$maxcolors, rownames(qual_col_pals)))

group_var <- "neighborhood"

plot_df <- meta_n %>%
  mutate(group = if (!is.null(group_var)) .data[[group_var]] else "all") %>%
  filter(!is.na(group), !is.na(num_genes), !is.na(num_rna_umi))

p_genes <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = group, y = num_genes, fill = group)) +
  geom_violin(scale = "width", trim = TRUE, linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = col_vector, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "num_genes") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

p_umi <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = group, y = num_rna_umi, fill = group)) +
  geom_violin(scale = "width", trim = TRUE, linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_y_log10() +   # log scale ONLY here
  scale_fill_manual(values = col_vector, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(x = "Neighborhood", y = "num_rna_umi (log10)") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

p_combined <- (p_genes / p_umi) + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1))
p_combined

ggsave(file.path(FIGURES_DIR, "Fig_1G.pdf"), plot = p_combined, dpi = 300, width = 7, height = 5, units = "in")

Fig 1I

# meta_n is now free of double-counted compound-neighborhood cells (fixed upstream above).
df <- meta_n %>%
  transmute(
    cell_type = .data[["neighborhood"]],
    n_guides   = .data[["num_guides"]]
  ) %>%
  mutate(n_guides_bin = case_when(
    n_guides == 0 ~ "0",
    n_guides == 1 ~ "1",
    n_guides == 2 ~ "2",
    n_guides > 2 ~ "3+",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )) %>%
  filter(!is.na(n_guides_bin)) %>%
  mutate(n_guides_bin = factor(n_guides_bin, levels = c("3+","2","1","0"))) %>%
  count(cell_type, n_guides_bin, name = "n") %>%
  group_by(cell_type) %>%
  mutate(pct = 100 * n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(df, aes(x = cell_type, y = pct, fill = n_guides_bin)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.9) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(n_guides_bin == "3+", "", paste0(round(pct, 1), ""))),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    angle = 90,
    size = 2
  ) +
  # FIX: fill legend was mislabelled "# genes" — corrected to "# guides"
  labs(x = "Neighborhood", y = "% of cells", fill = "# guides") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(x, "")) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

ggsave(file.path(FIGURES_DIR, "Fig_1I.pdf"), dpi = 300, width = 4, height = 4, units = "in")

Fig 1K

meta_sg <- meta_f %>%
  filter(num_guides == 1) %>%
  filter(!gene_target %in% "Non_target") %>%
  filter(!str_detect(gene_target, "^Safe_target"))

# Use predicted_group for perturbation × group pairs with >50 cells.
meta_sg$predicted_group_x_guide <- paste(meta_sg$predicted_group, meta_sg$gene_target, sep = " x ")
combo_counts <- table(meta_sg$predicted_group_x_guide)

df_counts <- as.data.frame(combo_counts)
colnames(df_counts) <- c("cell type x perturbation", "number of cells")
df_counts <- df_counts %>%
  arrange(desc(`number of cells`)) %>%
  mutate(rank = row_number()) %>%
  # Split "combo" into cell_type and perturbation columns, but keep "combo"
  separate(`cell type x perturbation`, into = c("cell_type", "perturbation"), sep = " x ", remove = FALSE)

write.csv(df_counts, file.path(BASE_DIR, "analysis", "visualization", "celltype_pert_number_of_cells.csv"), quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)

ggplot(df_counts, aes(x = rank, y = `number of cells`)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.8, alpha = 0.6) +
  # FIX: reference line at n=50 to reproduce the paper's ">50 cells" threshold
  geom_hline(yintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Combo rank (highest count → lowest)",
    y = "Number of cells (log10)",
    title = "Cells per predicted_group x guide (scatter)"
  )

ggsave(file.path(FIGURES_DIR, "Fig_1K.pdf"), dpi = 300, width = 7, height = 5, units = "in")
